content.text: Answer FOUR questions in detail. State carefully any results used without proof.

problem.text:
Let \((a_n)\) be a bounded sequence of strictly positive real numbers. Prove that
\[
\limsup (a_n^{1/n}) \leq \limsup (a_{n+1}/a_n).
\]
Hence prove that if \(a_{n+1}/a_n \to \ell \in \mathbb{R}\) then \(a_n^{1/n} \to \ell\) (as \(n \to \infty\)).

The diameter \(D\) of the bounded metric space \(X\) is defined by
\[
D=\sup\{d(a,b):a,b\in X\}.
\]
Prove that if \(X\) is compact then \(D=d(a_0,b_0)\) for some \(a_0,b_0\in X\).
The compact metric space \(X\) contains \(A_1\supseteq A_2\supseteq\cdots\) as a decreasing sequence of closed sets.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Prove that if each \(A_n\) is nonempty then so is the intersection \(A=\bigcap_{n=1}^{\infty}A_n\).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(U\subseteq X\) is open and contains \(A\) then \(U\) contains some \(A_n\).

problem.text: Let \(f:X\to Y\) be a map between metric spaces and consider the statements. Does (a) imply (b)? Does (b) imply (a)? In each case, give a proof if so, a counterexample if no.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: if \((x_n)\) is any Cauchy sequence in \(X\) then \((f(x_n))\) is Cauchy in \(Y\);
